text: Nasdaq Set to Open Down Amid Iran Peace Fears - Barron's
fullText: S&P 500 and Nasdaq futures are falling in premarket trading. The Dow is rising as the stock market digests Iran's peace offer.

text: Red Sox make additions to coaching staff in wake of dismissal of six coaches on Alex Cora’s staff - The Boston Globe
fullText: Pablo Cabrera, a coordinator who works with both infielders and outfielders, will join the team as a first base coach. Drew Ehrhard will join the big league team as a bullpen catcher.

text: 4 takeaways: Magic push Pistons to the brink in Game 4 - NBA
fullText: The Magic keep their mind right, Cade Cunningham can't find the openings and more from Orlando's pivotal Game 4 victory.

text: Kimmel Pushes Back Against White House And FLOTUS Criticism Of ‘Expectant Widow’ Joke - Forbes
fullText: Kimmel’s remarks about the shooting follow calls for his firing by President Donald Trump for a joke last week where he said Melania Trump had “a glow like an expectant widow.”

text: Pompeii archaeologists use AI to reconstruct man killed in volcano's eruption - NPR
fullText: Archaeologists have used AI for the first time to digitally reconstruct the face of a man killed in the AD 79 eruption of Mount Vesuvius, offering a new way to understand one of history's most famous natural disasters.

text: "DeSantis Dummymander": Democrats heap ridicule on Florida's proposed House maps - Axios
fullText: "Meatball Ron's Dummymander will not save Republicans," said one Democratic spokesperson.

text: Horoscope for Tuesday, April 28, 2026 - Chicago Sun-Times

text: General Motors is set to report earnings before the bell. Here's what Wall Street expects - CNBC
fullText: Aside from earnings and any change to GM's 2026 guidance, investors are monitoring impact from the Iran war, tariffs and EV write-downs.

text: Brain Scans Reveal a Surprise About Neanderthal Intelligence - ScienceAlert

text: Jury selection in Musk v. Altman: ‘People don’t like him’ - The Verge
fullText: Prospective jurors in the Elon Musk v. Sam Altman OpenAI trial called Musk a “jerk,” and “greedy.”

text: Violence escalates in Colombia with dozens of attacks before presidential vote - AP News
fullText: A series of attacks in Colombia's southwest has raised security concerns before the May presidential election. Rebel groups have staged 26 attacks since Friday. The death toll in a Saturday blast rose to 21 people on Monday. Authorities blame the FARC-EMC gro…

text: Kim Jong Un Confirms Soldier ‘Self-Blasting’ Policy in Ukraine - Bloomberg.com
fullText: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un confirmed a policy that requires soldiers to commit suicide on the battlefield to avoid capture, while fighting Russia’s war against Ukraine.

text: Hill Republicans lack a clear path to get Trump his ballroom - Politico
fullText: Only one Democrat so far has come out in favor of passing legislation that would continue construction of the stalled project after the Saturday night security breach.

text: Cooper Flagg wins NBA Rookie of the year over former Duke teammate Kon Knueppel - AP News
fullText: Dallas’ Cooper Flagg has edged former Duke teammate Kon Knueppel of Charlotte to win the NBA Rookie of the Year award. Flagg was the first rookie since Michael Jordan in 1984-85 to lead his team in points, rebounds, assists and steals. That and other accolade…

text: Congressional Republicans are split on using taxpayer funds to build Trump's ballroom - NBC News
fullText: Despite renewed GOP calls for funding the ballroom after a shooting at a dinner attended by the president, the project still faces an uphill climb in Congress.

text: Google is testing AI chatbot search for YouTube - The Verge
fullText: Google is trying out an AI Mode-like search called “Ask YouTube” that builds a result page with videos, Shorts, and text.

text: South Carolina's measles outbreak is over. But more are brewing around the country - NPR
fullText: The virus infected nearly 1,000 people in the state before the state declared it over. Meanwhile, cases are spreading across many parts of the U.S., with more than 20 outbreaks currently active.
